steps.title: Find the slope using the two known data points
steps.content:
From 1995 to 2015, the population grows from 59 thousand to 217 thousand. That's a span of $2015 - 1995 = 20$ years.

The slope is:
$$m = \frac{217 - 59}{2015 - 1995} = \frac{158}{20} = 7.9 \text{ thousand per year}$$

steps.title: Extend the line forward to 2019
steps.content:
From 2015 to 2019 is $2019 - 2015 = 4$ years.

Using the slope, the additional population growth is:
$$7.9 \times 4 = 31.6 \text{ thousand}$$

Add this to the 2015 population:
$$x = 217 + 31.6 = 248.6$$

Rounding to the nearest whole number, $x = 249$.
